methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Vertical Aerospace is a public-stage transportation company founded by Stephen Fitzpatrick that has raised $50M to date. The company operates within a sector populated by five identifiable private peers—Rivian, Einride, The Boring Company, Harbinger, and REGENT Craft—though no public comparables exist in the immediate transportation frontier-tech space. This scarcity of directly comparable public benchmarks and the fragmented maturity profiles across private peers constrain traditional valuation triangulation.

Peer-median methodology is not applicable here. The Transportation sector yields only two same-stage companies and zero adjacent-stage or similar-tier peers meeting minimum threshold requirements (n≥3). Attempting an all-sector median would conflate disparate business models and development stages, introducing material bias. The fundamental challenge is that frontier transportation remains sparsely populated at public scale, and the five private peers exhibit heterogeneous funding trajectories, technology architectures, and commercialization timelines that resist meaningful aggregation.

Given these constraints, a rigorous valuation cannot be triangulated from the supplied data. Forward-looking analysis would require either: (1) expansion of the comparable set to adjacent sectors with similar capital intensity and technology risk profiles, (2) revenue or unit economics multiples tied to Vertical's own operational milestones, or (3) DCF methodology anchored to disclosed production roadmaps and customer commitments. Current data supports valuation discussion only in qualitative terms: a capital-intensive, pre-revenue or early-revenue transportation hardware company at public stage occupies a structurally different risk profile than mature transportation peers.
